name: New 15A circuit (up to 25 ft)
description: Install a new dedicated 15A circuit from the panel to a new receptacle location (Up to 25 ft), including breaker, cable, box, and device.
labor_notes: Covers panel work, cable routing up to 25 ft, and device trim. Short home runs near the panel are the baseline: about half fishing, half terminations.

name: New 15A circuit (25-50 ft)
description: Install a new dedicated 15A circuit from the panel to a new receptacle location (25-50 ft), including breaker, cable, box, and device.
labor_notes: Covers panel work, cable routing 25-50 ft, and device trim. The longer run adds drilling and fishing time through additional bays.

name: New 15A circuit (50-100 ft)
description: Install a new dedicated 15A circuit from the panel to a new receptacle location (50-100 ft), including breaker, cable, box, and device.
labor_notes: Covers panel work, cable routing 50-100 ft, and device trim. Cross-house runs usually traverse attic or crawl; allow for access setup at both ends.

name: New 20A circuit (up to 25 ft)
description: Install a new dedicated 20A circuit from the panel to a new receptacle location (Up to 25 ft), including breaker, cable, box, and device.
labor_notes: Covers panel work, cable routing up to 25 ft, and device trim. Same routing effort as 15A; 12 AWG terminations are slightly stiffer to work.

name: New 20A circuit (25-50 ft)
description: Install a new dedicated 20A circuit from the panel to a new receptacle location (25-50 ft), including breaker, cable, box, and device.
labor_notes: Covers panel work, cable routing 25-50 ft, and device trim. The longer run adds drilling and fishing time through additional bays.

name: New 20A circuit (50-100 ft)
description: Install a new dedicated 20A circuit from the panel to a new receptacle location (50-100 ft), including breaker, cable, box, and device.
labor_notes: Covers panel work, cable routing 50-100 ft, and device trim. Long 12 AWG pulls across the structure; check panel-side routing space early.

name: Dedicated dishwasher circuit
description: Install a dedicated 20A circuit for a dishwasher, routed to the sink cabinet area.
labor_notes: Kitchen cabinet routing is tighter than open-wall work; includes GFCI protection per current code via breaker or device per layout.

name: Dedicated disposal circuit with switch
description: Install a dedicated 20A disposal circuit with a countertop-height switch and sink-cabinet receptacle.
labor_notes: Adds a switch leg over the plain appliance circuit; cabinet and backsplash routing set the pace.

name: Dedicated microwave circuit
description: Install a dedicated 20A circuit for an over-range or countertop microwave.
labor_notes: Upper-cabinet receptacle placement means working above the range area; otherwise a standard dedicated circuit.

name: Dedicated refrigerator circuit
description: Install a dedicated 20A circuit for the refrigerator.
labor_notes: Usually a straightforward run to an accessible wall cavity behind the fridge; slightly quicker than cabinet-bound circuits.

name: Dedicated laundry (washer) circuit
description: Install a dedicated 20A laundry-area circuit per code.
labor_notes: Laundry areas are often adjacent to the panel or garage, but GFCI requirements and finished-wall fishing keep this at three hours.

name: Kitchen small-appliance circuit pair
description: Install the two code-required 20A small-appliance circuits serving kitchen countertop receptacles.
labor_notes: Two full circuits with GFCI protection and backsplash boxes; counted together because they share routing paths and setup.

name: GFCI receptacle retrofit (first location)
description: Replace a standard receptacle with a GFCI receptacle at one location (kitchen, bath, garage, or exterior).
labor_notes: Half an hour covers verification of line/load, the swap, and test; first location carries the setup time.

name: GFCI receptacle retrofit (each additional)
description: Each additional GFCI receptacle replaced during the same visit.
labor_notes: Marginal per-device time once tools are staged and the circuit map is known.

name: GFCI breaker retrofit
description: Protect an entire circuit by replacing its breaker with a GFCI breaker at the panel.
labor_notes: Panel work plus locating the circuit's neutral; includes trip-testing downstream devices.

name: AFCI breaker retrofit (first)
description: Replace a standard breaker with a combination AFCI breaker to add arc-fault protection to a circuit.
labor_notes: Swap plus neutral relocation and a nuisance-trip check with typical loads on the circuit.

name: AFCI breaker retrofit (each additional)
description: Each additional AFCI breaker installed during the same panel session.
labor_notes: Marginal time per breaker with the panel already open and mapped.

name: Dual-function AFCI/GFCI breaker retrofit
description: Replace a standard breaker with a dual-function AFCI/GFCI breaker for combined protection.
labor_notes: Same labor as an AFCI swap; the dual-function device carries the cost difference.

name: Whole-panel AFCI retrofit (up to 10 circuits)
description: Upgrade up to ten branch circuits to AFCI protection in one panel session, with load testing on each.
labor_notes: Batching ten breaker swaps with neutral relocations and per-circuit testing; obsolete panels change the math and should be flagged.

name: USB charging receptacle swap (first)
description: Replace a standard receptacle with a duplex receptacle that includes USB-A/USB-C charging ports.
labor_notes: USB devices are deeper than standard receptacles — box fill check included in the half hour.

name: USB charging receptacle swap (each additional)
description: Each additional USB receptacle installed during the same visit.
labor_notes: Marginal per-device time once staged.

name: Switch to dimmer swap (single-pole)
description: Replace a standard switch with an LED-compatible dimmer.
labor_notes: Includes verifying fixture load compatibility with the dimmer; LED flicker checks add a few minutes.

name: Switch to dimmer swap (3-way)
description: Replace a 3-way switch with an LED-compatible 3-way dimmer.
labor_notes: Traveler identification on older wiring takes the extra quarter hour over a single-pole swap.

name: New 3-way switching (two locations)
description: Add 3-way switching so a light can be controlled from two locations, including new switch and traveler cable.
labor_notes: Fishing 14/3 traveler cable between two switch points through finished walls is the bulk of the time.

name: Exterior receptacle add
description: Add a weather-resistant GFCI receptacle with in-use cover on an exterior wall, fed from an interior circuit or new run.
labor_notes: Back-to-back through the rim or wall from an interior box keeps this at 1.5 hours; sealing the penetration properly matters.

name: Garage receptacle add (surface EMT)
description: Add a 20A receptacle in a garage using surface-mounted EMT from an existing circuit or the panel.
labor_notes: Exposed EMT in a garage is fast, honest work: no fishing, just neat bends and anchors.

name: Receptacle add on existing circuit
description: Add a new receptacle fed from a nearby existing receptacle or junction point.
labor_notes: Short fish from an adjacent box; load check on the existing circuit is part of the job.

name: Floor receptacle add
description: Install a floor box receptacle for furniture placement away from walls, fed from below or an adjacent wall.
labor_notes: Cutting flooring cleanly and routing from below (crawl or basement) makes this slower than a wall receptacle.

name: Smart switch install
description: Replace a standard switch with a Wi-Fi smart switch, including neutral verification and app pairing handoff.
labor_notes: Neutral availability check is the gating factor; includes pairing the device and confirming remote control works.

name: Timer switch install
description: Install an electronic countdown timer switch, typically for a bath fan or exterior lighting.
labor_notes: Simple device swap plus programming and a walkthrough with the homeowner.
